description: I2C Tools is the primary suite for scanning, probing, and pushing explicit byte transmissions natively over the Inter-Integrated Circuit embedded components hardware bus.

commands.explanation: Brute-force probes the designated I2C master bus (-y to bypass warnings) returning a grid establishing the explicit hexadecimal addresses of all physically wired responsive slave sensors.
Prints out the absolute system list of all installed operational I2C buses (e.g., i2c-1, i2c-0) inherently available on the SoC.
Taps into a defined slave chip address on a specified bus attempting to extract and display its immediate default resting value state.
Extracts explicitly targeted byte responses natively from a strict memory data register physically embedded inside the slave IC.
Forces an absolute mass readout visually dumping every single stored byte value mapped across every single register array inside the target peripheral.
Hardwrites a specific absolute numerical value natively directly into a unique designated structural register embedded inside the IC.
Fires contiguous high-speed multi-byte transaction arrays forcefully feeding sequence logics across the bridge to initialize complex displays or EEPROMs.
Analyzes the deeply loaded kernel structures explicitly verifying that the `i2c_bcm2835` and `i2c_dev` master controller drivers are actually actively bridged.
Queries a specific hardware bus printing an absolute list detailing precisely which complex I2C algorithms and SMBus transaction variants it formally supports.
Modifies data retrieval logic forcing the controller to fetch an entire absolute 16-bit word length (w) instead of stopping natively after a single standard 8-bit byte.
